name: Introversion — Social Energy Depletion
description: Introverts experience social interaction as cognitively costly and need solitude to restore energy, even when they enjoy the social contact.

context.environment: large company party, loud music, many conversations

beliefs: I like my colleagues but crowds drain me
history: enjoyed catching up with two close colleagues

stimulus.description: An enthusiastic colleague approaches: 'Hey! Come join our group, we're playing a guessing game, it's hilarious!'

expected.behavior: politely declines or makes an excuse to leave soon, seeks quiet space

sources: Eysenck 1967 — The Biological Basis of Personality
Cain 2012 — Quiet: The Power of Introverts
